 Som en del av vårt växande ERP-erbjudande blir du en central spelare i projekt som spänner från omfattande ERP-transformationer till integrerade lösningar där Business Central samverkar med Power Platform, Dynamics CE, Azure och andra Microsoft-teknologier.
   

  

**Dina arbetsuppgifter**
 I rollen ansvarar du bland annat för att:
   

  

* Leda eller driva projekt och implementationer genom hela livscykeln – från analys och planering till genomförande, uppföljning och vidareutveckling
* Vara rådgivande partner till kunder i frågor kopplade till verksamhetsprocesser och affärssystem
* Skapa struktur, tydlighet och framdrift i komplexa leveranser med flera intressenter
* Arbeta nära utvecklare, arkitekter och andra konsulter i tvärfunktionella team
* Bidra till att vidareutveckla kundrelationer och identifiera nya affärsmöjligheter
* Samordna implementationer och integrationer kopplade till Business Central och närliggande lösningar


**Vi söker dig som**
* Har minst 5 års erfarenhet av Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central eller andra ERP-lösningar
* Idag arbetar som exempelvis projektledare, applikationskonsult eller verksamhetskonsult inom ERP
* Har erfarenhet av implementationer, verksamhetsutveckling eller affärssystemprojekt, gärna i konsultverksamhet
* Är van att arbeta nära kund och känner dig trygg i dialoger med både verksamhet och IT
* Har god förståelse för affärsprocesser inom exempelvis ekonomi, logistik, lager eller produktion
* Trivs i en rådgivande och drivande roll där du får kombinera affär och teknik
* Har förmåga att skapa struktur och framdrift i komplexa leveranser
* Kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska och engelska, i både tal och skrift


 Erfarenhet av Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central är särskilt meriterande. Vi ser också gärna att du har arbetat med andra etablerade ERP-plattformar, såsom Jeeves, Pyramid, IFS, Monitor, Visma eller liknande lösningar.
   

  

**Varför Sopra Steria?**
 Hos oss får du arbeta tillsammans med några av marknadens främsta experter inom affärssystem och digital transformation. Vi erbjuder spännande kunduppdrag, kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling och goda karriärmöjligheter i ett bolag där människan alltid står i centrum.
   

  

* Bli en del av ett toppteam: Arbeta sida vid sida med erfarna specialister inom Microsoft Dynamics 365 och ERP.
* Utvecklas på riktigt: Vi satsar på certifieringar, ledarskapsutveckling och personlig utveckling.
* Projekt som utmanar: Våra kunder finns inom flera olika branscher och våra projekt varierar i både komplexitet och omfattning.
* En kultur där människor trivs: Vi är stolta över en arbetsmiljö där samarbete, innovation och balans står i fokus.

title: Category Development Leader
description:
Job Title
   

  

 Category Development Leader
   

  

**Job Description**
**Category Development Leader – Personal Health (NORDICS)**
**Location:** 
 Stockholm, Sweden
   

  

**About The Role**
 As the
 *Category Development Leader Personal Health NORDICS* 
 , you will lead the Shopper Marketing function for Sweden, Norway, Finland and Denmark. In this highly cross-functional role, you will collaborate closely with Consumer Marketing, Key Account Management, Category Development Manager and major retail partners to elevate category growth, drive brand visibility, and deliver impactful omnichannel shopper experiences.
   

  

 You will oversee strategic category development for a broad Personal Health portfolio, including:
   

  

* Oral Healthcare (Philips Sonicare)
* Male Grooming (incl. Philips OneBlade)
* Beauty (Lumea IPL, haircare appliances)
* Mother & Child Care (Philips Avent)


 Join an empowered team where you’ll own meaningful work from day one. You’ll be trusted to decide, supported to deliver, and surrounded by colleagues who are
 **open, collaborative, and fun to work with** 
 . We operate in an
 **international environment, with career paths across the Nordics and beyond** 
 . If you’re accountable, curious, and driven—and you want both autonomy and support—this is where you’ll grow.
   

  

**Your Responsibilities**
**Strategic Leadership**
* Translate marketing objectives into compelling customer strategies and localized shopper activation plans that drive value for consumers, retailers, and the business.
* Shape the omnichannel vision across key categories and retail environments.


**Team Leadership**
* Lead and develop a team of Shopper Activation Managers and Shopper Excellence Manager
* Build capabilities through coaching, knowledge sharing, and continuous improvement.


**Shopper Marketing Excellence**
* Own the development and implementation of NORDICS-wide shopper activation plans across all channels: Consumer Electronics Retail, Drug & Grocery, and Online Pure Players.
* Increase brand presence through best‑in‑class in‑store and online activation, including retail media and POS excellence.
* Drive promotional efficiency and coordination across retailers and categories.


**Performance & Results**
* Ensure high-quality execution of omnichannel campaigns to accelerate category growth and market share.
* Track performance through relevant KPIs and optimize strategies based on actionable insights.


**Cross-Functional & External Collaboration**
* Partner with Commercial, Marketing, Regional Shopper Marketing, and Business Unit teams.
* Act as a strategic advisor to retail partners and position the company as a preferred innovation partner.
* Manage external agencies and suppliers to deliver high-impact campaigns.


**Budget Ownership**
* Manage the Shopper Marketing A&P budget, ensuring optimal allocation across in-store and digital touchpoints.


**Your Profile**
**Experience & Skills**
* Several years of experience in Shopper Marketing, Trade Marketing, or Category Management, ideally in FMCG or consumer goods.
* Proven leadership experience with a passion for developing teams.
* Strong commercial mindset and experience managing omnichannel campaigns.
* Excellent analytical skills and proficiency in Excel and analytics tools.
* Strategic thinker with the ability to translate insight into action.
* Strong communicator with confidence when dealing with internal stakeholders and retail partners.
* Highly collaborative, proactive, and detail-oriented.


**Education**
* Master's degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or a comparable field.


**Languages**
* Fluent in English and minimum one other language of the Nordic markets (written and spoken).


**Personal Qualities**
* Creative mindset and strong trend awareness.
* Deep understanding of consumer and shopper behavior.
* Team-oriented with a drive for process improvement and operational excellence.


**You Will Be Part Of**
 A dynamic
 **Personal Health** 
 organization driving online and in‑store activation across Nordics. You and your team will bridge Product Marketing and Sales — making you a key driver of category growth and shopper conversion in one of the most strategic business areas.
   

  

**How We Work Together**
 We believe that we are better together than apart. For our office-based teams, this means working in-person at least 3 days per week.
   

  

 This is an office role.
   

  

**About Philips**
 We are a health technology company. We built our entire company around the belief that every human matters, and we won't stop until everybody everywhere has access to the quality healthcare that we all deserve. Do the work of your life to help the lives of others.
   

  

* Learn more about our business.
* Discover our rich and exciting history.
* Learn more about our purpose.


 If you’re interested in this role and have many, but not all, of the experiences needed, we encourage you to apply. You may still be the right candidate for this or other opportunities at Philips. Learn more about our culture of impact with care here.
   

  

 #PersonalHealth

title: Digital designer med AD-kompetens till Naturvårdsverket
description:
Digital designer med AD-kompetens till Naturvårdsverket

**Sök jobbet**
--------------

### **Placeringsort**


Stockholm eller Östersund

### **Anställningsform**


Tillsvidare

### **Sista ansökningsdatum**


2026-06-22

 *Naturvårdsverket driver och samordnar arbetet för en god livsmiljö tillsammans med andra. Hos oss får du vara med och bidra i det viktiga arbetet för att nå vår vision - att skapa en bra livsmiljö för människan och allt annat levande, nu och för kommande generationer!*


Vill du designa lösningar som bidrar till att minska klimatpåverkan, bevara biologisk mångfald och skapa ett resurseffektivt samhälle? Vi söker en digital designer med AD-kompetens som vill ta en aktiv roll i att forma vårt digitala uttryck och tillsammans med duktiga kollegor och leverantörer skapa lösningar som är användarvänliga, tillgängliga och visuellt sammanhållna.


Tjänsten är placerad på enheten för digitala kanaler som är en del av kommunikationsavdelningen. Vi arbetar med att styra, utveckla och följa upp användningen av Naturvårdsverkets digitala kanaler. Enheten stödjer hela organisationen i webbpublicering och ansvarar för Naturvårdsverkets visuella identitet.

**Dina arbetsuppgifter**
------------------------

I rollen som Digital designer med AD-kompetens ansvarar du, i nära samarbete med andra roller, för att ta fram och vidareutveckla visuella koncept och designlösningar för våra digitala kanaler. Arbetet omfattar både strategiskt och operativt designarbete från idé och koncept till färdig produktion och kvalitetssäkring. Du samarbetar med exempelvis utvecklare, handläggare, webbredaktörer, digital strateg och externa leverantörer. Framåt förväntas uppdraget utvecklas mot ett tydligare helhetsansvar för det digitala uttrycket, ökad användarcentrering, fortsatt utveckling av designprocesser och arbetssätt samt ett övergripande ansvar för myndighetens designsystem.

**Din kompetens**
-----------------

### **Krav**

* Eftergymnasial utbildning inom grafisk design, digital design, visuell kommunikation eller webbdesign
* Dokumenterad utbildning eller motsvarande erfarenhet inom design för digitala gränssnitt (UI)
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet som digital designer, webbdesigner eller Art Director
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av design för webb och digitala plattformar
* Erfarenhet av att ta fram designkoncept från idé till färdig leverans
* Erfarenhet av att agera beställare av grafisk formgivning, digitala design- och webbtjänster, inklusive kravställning, briefarbete och uppföljning av leveranser från externa leverantörer/byråer.
* God vana av designverktyg som Figma och/eller Adobe Creative Cloud
* Erfarenhet av att leda eller kvalitetssäkra designarbete samt grafisk formgivning
### **Meriterande**

* Utbildning inom UX/-design eller interaktionsdesign
* Erfarenhet av att utveckla och förvalta designsystem
* Erfarenhet av att utveckla och förvalta en visuell identitet
* Erfarenhet av att samarbeta i agila team
* Grundläggande förståelse för HTML/CSS och tekniska förutsättningar i webb
* Erfarenhet av upphandling
* Erfarenhet av att ha arbetat inom offentlig sektor
* Erfarenhet av att visualisera statistik och data
**Personliga egenskaper**
-------------------------


Vi söker dig som är kommunikativ och har lätt för att samarbeta med olika kompetenser, både internt och externt. Du är formstark och har en god förmåga att skapa lösningar med ett tydligt och genomtänkt visuellt uttryck. Du arbetar strukturerat och kan hålla ihop designprocesser från idé till färdig leverans. Samtidigt är du självgående och driver ditt arbete framåt, tar initiativ och ansvar för att nå resultat.


Vi vill dessutom också att du är nyfiken och håller dig uppdaterad kring nya trender, verktyg och arbetssätt inom digital design.


Vi lägger stor vikt vid de personliga egenskaperna.

**Om anställningen**
--------------------


Anställningen är en tillsvidaretjänst på heltid. Placering är i Stockholm eller Östersund. Tillträde sker snarast enligt överenskommelse. Vi tillämpar individuell lönesättning.

**Ansökan**
-----------


När du ansöker får du besvara ett antal urvalsfrågor som är kopplade till kravprofilen i annonsen. Dina svar används som en del av urvalsarbetet och kan avgöra om du går vidare i rekryteringsprocessen eller inte. Därför är det viktigt att du svarar så utförligt och tydligt som möjligt. Vi använder oss inte av personligt brev vid urvalet, då vi vill lägga fokus på din erfarenhet och kompetens för en mer effektiv och rättvis rekryteringsprocess. I din ansökan till oss bifogar du endast ditt CV.


Varmt välkommen med din ansökan **senast den 22 juni 2026**.

**Kontakt**
-----------


Vill du veta mer om anställningen är du välkommen att kontakta Elma Jakupovic Holtsjö. Du kan även kontakta våra fackliga företrädare för Saco-S eller ST. Samtliga nås via kundtjänst, tel.nr. 010-698 10 00.

**Varför ska du arbeta hos oss?**
---------------------------------


Naturvårdsverket värnar om ett hållbart arbetsliv och en god arbetsmiljö. Vi erbjuder våra medarbetare goda möjligheter till distansarbete, företagshälsovård och personalstöd, friskvårdstimme och friskvårdsbidrag, samt olika aktiviteter via vår sport- och friluftsförening och kulturförening. Välkommen till våra moderna kontor i en organisation med ett viktigt uppdrag. Vi är en arbetsplats med trevliga kollegor och ett närvarande ledarskap där vi samlas kring vårt övergripande uppdrag som syftar till att förbättra miljön för nuvarande och kommande generationer.

**Naturvårdsverket växer – vill du vara med och bidra?**
--------------------------------------------------------


Naturvårdsverket befinner sig i en spännande utvecklingsfas. Regeringen har aviserat ökade anslag och nya uppdrag, vilket ger oss möjlighet att investera för framtiden. Därför söker vi nya medarbetare inom flera områden. Hos oss får du möjlighet att bidra till ett hållbart och robust samhälle.

**Om Naturvårdsverket**
-----------------------


Naturvårdsverket är en statlig myndighet för klimat- och miljöfrågor. Vi arbetar på uppdrag av regeringen inom Sverige, inom EU och internationellt. Naturvårdsverket driver och samordnar miljöarbetet i Sverige. Vi bidrar till ett robust och hållbart samhälle.


Naturvårdsverket ansvarar för frågor som handlar om klimat och luft, mark, biologisk mångfald, förorenade områden, kretslopp och avfall, resurseffektivitet, miljöövervakning, jakt och vilt samt miljöforskning. Myndighetens ansvarsområden är integrerade i andra viktiga samhällsfrågor och bidrar därför till hela samhällets utveckling. Naturvårdsverket arbetar också för att stärka samhällets beredskap.


Naturvårdsverket eftersträvar en arbetsplats präglad av jämställdhet och mångfald. Vi ser att medarbetare med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter är en viktig förutsättning för detta.


Inför rekryteringsarbetet har vi tagit ställning till rekryteringskanaler och marknadsföring och undanber oss därför kontakt med mediesäljare, rekryteringssajter och liknande.

title: UX Designer
description:
As a UX Designer you will be working on one of our Game Projects in a cross-disciplinary team, taking ownership for UX design of features to improve the end-user experience. You will be part of shipping new features, and balancing maintenance of existing features for live service games.
  
To thrive in this role we think you have experience working with complex products and designing for different platforms and input methods such as PC, console or mobile. Previous experience in working with games is a plus.
  
Example of responsibilities
  
  

Collaborate across disciplines and align stakeholders to define clear problem statements, user needs and design solutions to solve those.
  
  

Own the user experience of various features end-to-end, from defining problems and needs iterating all the way until they are in the players’ hands.
  
  

Design using user flows, wireframes and prototypes, that adhere to the game’s design system and style guides.
  
  

Work on a range of experiences, from crafting systems to character customization, based on where we can have the biggest impact.
  
  

Work proactively, identifying gaps in the user experience to inform design decisions
  
  

Consider systems before individual screens. Consider edge cases early, flexible design solutions, and aim for coherency in design choices.
  
  

Deliver and explore many possible solutions, while able to narrow down and pivot with relevant feedback.
  
  

Contribute and foster a culture of sharing and feedback on design.
  
  

Leverage the team’s combined expertise in finding the best solutions and increase the knowledge across the studio.
  
  

We would love if you have
  
  

A creative and curious mind motivated by understanding user needs and how to fill those to improve the experience.
  
  

Strong communication skills to clearly convey design decisions, and doing so early and frequently under development.
  
  

Experience in using both qualitative and quantitative data to understand user needs and finding possible design solutions.
  
  

Experience with different platforms and input methods, such as focus-based input.
  
  

Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with shifting priorities for the biggest impact.
  
  

An interest in games and player experiences.
  
  

4+ years of experience in UX/UI or product design.
  
  

A portfolio with relevant projects showcasing your design process and impact.
  
  

Fluency in English.
  
  

At Embark we offer competitive salaries, passionate colleagues to share knowledge with and much more, but most of all we invite you to take part of a journey into the unknown, to build creative, surprising and beautiful experiences together.
  
Be yourself at Embark and make games while doing so. Please apply with confidence. We can’t wait to hear from you (in English)!

title: Digital Creative Strategist
description:
**Company Description**  

Commercial Development & Growth is a global function within the H&M Brand. Our mission is to define and deliver the global customer experience strategy - strengthening our brand while enabling profitable growth. Together, we develop, expand and optimise all sales channels, creating the conditions for efficient, tech-enabled operations through strong cross-functional collaboration.


We play a key role in bringing the brand plan to life by shaping an elevated, inspirational and relevant shopping experience. By putting our product at the heart of the journey, we ensure a seamless and competitive experience for our customers.


Within this, the Creative Development department delivers a cohesive brand experience across all channels - anchored in our identity and guided by a clear, consistent and creative vision.

 **Job Description** **WHAT YOU’LL DO**


We’re now looking for a Digital Creative Strategist to join the Creative Development team and shape the direction of our digital store experience. In this role, you’ll connect strategy with creativity - turning ambition into clear, compelling creative concepts and ideas that drive brand impact, user engagement and commercial success.

*You will:*

* Frame ambition into clear creative direction
	+ Develop clear creative briefs that clarify scope, ambition, and priorities
	+ Translate strategic input into actionable creative direction that guides digital initiative
	+ Define experience principles and creative guardrails with the overall brand direction
* Shape the creative ambition and expression
	+ Develop core creative concepts, narrative direction, and emotional ambition for key initiatives
	+ Translate creative intent into clear design principles guiding visual identity and storytelling
	+ Make informed creative recommendations balancing brand ambition, user experience, and commercial impact
* Secure quality and coherence in execution
	+ Apply and uphold creative principles to ensure consistency and distinctiveness across products and platforms
	+ Provide creative leadership to internal teams and external partners during concept and design development
	+ Ensure execution aligns with the defined creative direction

  

**Qualifications**  

WHO YOU ARE

*We are looking for people with…*

* 8+ years experience in digital creative or design roles
* A degree in Design, Communication, Strategy, or a related field
* High design judgement across digital UI/UX and visual systems
* The ability to balance brand ambition, usability, and commercial impact
* Clear communication skills across Creative, Product, and Commercial teams

*And people who are…*

* Confident in shaping both strategic framing and creative direction within product-led environments
* Collaborative, with a proactive and solution-focused mindset
* Comfortable leading creative conversations and influencing stakeholders
* Strategic thinkers and conceptually strong

  

**Additional Information**  

This is a **full time permanent position** based in Stockholm at our offices. Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through our career page.

**WHO WE ARE**

H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.


We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future.
 

**WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE**  

At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe.


* 25% Staff discount on all our H&M Group brands, both in stores and online (H&M, COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories and ARKET).
* H&M Incentive Program – HIP. Learn more about the program here.
* In addition to our global benefits, our local markets offer different competitive perks and benefits. (Example: Competitive compensation for your dedication and long-term commitment. A day off on your birthday to relax and celebrate your special day. Special gift to celebrate the birth of your child, etc.)

**JOIN US**

Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.


Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.


We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.
company_description: At H&M, we welcome you to be yourself and challenge you to grow and make a difference. Help us reimagine the future of an entire industry, making everyone look, feel & do good.

title: UX-designer till ett spännande konsultuppdrag hos vår kund!
description:
Uppdraget passar dig som vill arbeta verksamhetsnära med användarcentrerad design och tillgängliga digitala lösningar i komplexa miljöer där kvalitet, användbarhet och struktur står i fokus.


I rollen får du arbeta genom hela utvecklingsprocessen – från behovsanalys och användarinsikter till design, prototypframtagning, användningstester och kvalitetssäkring av färdiga lösningar. Uppdraget innebär nära samarbete med utvecklare, verksamhet, beställare och andra intressenter i flera parallella initiativ.


Om uppdraget  

Som UX-designer kommer du att bidra till utvecklingen av digitala tjänster och system med höga krav på användbarhet, tillgänglighet och funktionalitet. Uppdragen kan omfatta flera olika verksamhetsområden, exempelvis vård, HR och ekonomi, vilket innebär varierande målgrupper och användarbehov.


Du arbetar självständigt och förväntas kunna driva flera uppdrag parallellt med olika omfattning och komplexitet.


I uppdraget ingår bland annat att:

* Genomföra användarstudier och behovsanalyser
* Identifiera och omsätta verksamhetsbehov till krav och designlösningar
* Ta fram wireframes, interaktionsdesign och klickbara prototyper
* Planera och genomföra användningstester och workshops
* Säkerställa att lösningar uppfyller tillgänglighetskrav enligt WCAG
* Arbeta med och vidareutveckla designsystem
* Samarbeta nära utvecklare, produktägare, verksamhet och andra intressenter
* Bidra till förbättring av användarupplevelse och digitala arbetssätt


Vi söker dig som har:

* Relevant utbildning inom exempelvis interaktionsdesign, kognitionsvetenskap, teknikinformation eller motsvarande
* Erfarenhet av UX-/interaktionsdesign genom hela utvecklingsprocessen
* Erfarenhet av prototypframtagning och användningstester
* God kunskap inom tillgänglighet och WCAG
* Erfarenhet av Figma eller liknande design- och prototypingverktyg
* Förmåga att arbeta självständigt och driva flera initiativ parallellt
* Mycket god kommunikativ förmåga på svenska, både i tal och skrift


För att vara aktuell för uppdraget behöver du:

* Vara svensk medborgare
* Behärska svenska på nivå C1 eller motsvarande
* Kunna arbeta självständigt och ta ansvar för egna delområden


Meriterande erfarenheter:

* Arbete med vårdrelaterade system eller offentlig verksamhet
* Erfarenhet av design för flera olika målgrupper, exempelvis vårdpersonal
* Erfarenhet av avancerad tillgänglighetsanpassning enligt WCAG
* Erfarenhet av att bygga upp och förvalta designsystem


Personliga egenskaper  

Vi tror att du är en strukturerad och lösningsorienterad person med ett starkt användarfokus. Du trivs i samarbete med olika kompetenser och har förmåga att omsätta komplexa behov till tydliga och användarvänliga lösningar. Du är kommunikativ, nyfiken och van att ta eget ansvar i ditt arbete.

**Låter detta som din nästa utmaning?**


Vad kul! Skicka in din ansökan så hör vi av oss för ett nästa steg i processen.


Som konsult hos oss på Job Solution Consulting omfattas man av en trygg anställning i nära dialog med sin konsultchef för att alltid säkerställa att du trivs och utvecklas i din roll. Vi erbjuder en rad förmåner med fokus på kompetensutveckling och välmående, Och självklart omfattas man också av kollektivavtal i sin tjänst hos oss. För eventuella frågor eller funderingar är du välkommen att höra av dig till mig.


Du når mig på fredrik.hovstadius@jobsolution.se.

title: Konsultuppdrag | Senior React Native
description:
**Office policy:** Stockholm. 4 days a week in the office

**Availability:** Preferably as soon as possible


An established and profitable fintech scale-up is looking for a **Senior React Native Engineer** to help drive our mobile platform forward. Having recently achieved major regulatory milestones, we are navigating exciting technical challenges and expanding our product ecosystem.


We operate with a high-performance, **in-office culture** because we believe close collaboration sparks the best innovation. If you thrive in a fast-paced environment with high autonomy and zero corporate bureaucracy, this is for you.

### **The Role: High Autonomy, Real Impact**


We build lean. We don't scale by adding headcount; we scale by keeping the engineering bar exceptionally high. As a core member of our mobile team, you won't get lost in a massive organization—your decisions will directly shape our product and technical architecture.

### **What You'll Do**

* **Lead Mobile Development:** Take ownership of our core iOS and Android applications, shipping high-quality features using React Native.
* **Architecture & Scalability:** Design modular components, clean abstractions, and scalable mobile architectures that improve developer experience.
* **UI/UX Collaboration:** Work hand-in-hand with product designers to implement highly polished, accessible, and responsive interfaces.
* **API Integration:** Connect mobile frontends with modern, data-driven backend APIs to ensure seamless performance.
* **Experimentation & Optimization:** Run feature flags, A/B tests, and iterations to optimize user engagement and retention.
* **Quality & Mentorship:** Maintain solid testing practices and mentor junior/mid-level peers to elevate the team's collective output.

### **Our Engineering Philosophy**


We value pragmatism, speed, and craftsmanship. We don't believe in doing things the hard way just for the sake of it. **AI is deeply integrated into our daily workflow** to eliminate friction, automate routine tasks, and allow you to focus on complex problem-solving. We leave egos at the door—what matters is good judgment and high execution.

### **Tech Stack**


Our ecosystem centers around **React Native, TypeScript, and modern API gateways (GraphQL/REST)**, alongside modern tools for UI documentation and feature experimentation.

### **Who You Are**

* **Experienced:** You have a proven track record of building and deploying production-ready mobile apps with React Native.
* **Product-Minded:** You care about the user experience, performance, and fine details, not just the underlying code.
* **Proactive:** You take extreme ownership of your work and don’t wait for permission to fix a problem or improve a system.
* **Collaborative:** You enjoy brainstorming with cross-functional peers and value the energy of working alongside your team in the office.

 **Om oss**

*Senterprise är ett ledande rekryteringsbolag inom IT och en oberoende förmedlare av frilansande konsulter. Med aktiv headhunting, search och djup teknisk förståelse hjälper vi några av Sveriges främsta arbetsgivare att hitta rätt kandidater, både för anställning och konsultuppdrag.*

*Läs vidare på* *www.senterprise.se*

title: Full-Stack Engineer for Elskling and Insplanet
description:
We're hiring a developer for our Power & Protect business unit, home to Elskling and Insplanet. Here, you won't just write code — you'll ship features that help thousands of users make smarter decisions about their energy and insurance every single day.


We believe in removing friction from development. That means modern tooling like Claude Code for AI-assisted development, automated workflows that handle the boring stuff, and a culture where shipping beats meetings. Your ideas go from concept to production fast — and you'll see the impact in real time.


If you want to own outcomes, not just tasks, this is your team.

**About the Role**


You’ll join an experienced tech team that moves fast and ships often. Small team, real influence: your decisions shape the product, and you'll see results in days, not months.


You'll work cross-functionally with Product, UX, marketing, and business development — close enough to understand why you're building, not just what. You'll also collaborate across Axo's broader distributed tech organization as we scale.


We're agile and data-driven: continuous integration, TDD, and pair programming are how we work, not buzzwords on a slide. Full-stack experience is a plus, but what matters most is that you're curious and ready to grow. AI is our multiplier, not our autopilot — we're actively integrating it into how we build, with humans driving every decision.

**Our Tech Stack**


Modern JavaScript at the core: Node, Vue.js, and TypeScript in a component-driven architecture. Everything runs on AWS, and the team owns the full lifecycle: you build it, you ship it, you monitor it. No handoffs, no waiting.

**Relevant experience includes:**

* JavaScript, TypeScript
* Node.js, Vue.js
* AWS, Terraform
* MySQL, SQL
* GitHub

**About You**


You are a quality-driven, creative, and solution-oriented developer who thrives in a team where collaboration is key. You have experience with multiple programming languages, cloud environments, and deployment workflows, preferably using AWS. You bring at least three years of relevant software development experience.


You’re comfortable working in an agile setup with stand-ups, sprints, tickets, continuous releases and retrospectives. You communicate well in both Swedish and English. You have a relevant educational background, and a strong interest in software and web development. You enjoy improving, maintaining, and evolving existing products, and you’re happy to share new ideas, perspectives, and energy with the team.

 **What We Offer**


We combine high pace with a flexible and caring work environment. We value making a real difference for our customers, while having fun together along the way.


Axo Group is the company behind Axo, LendMe, Zmarta, uScore, Elskling, and Freedom Finance —helping millions of Nordic consumers take control of their personal finances. 180 employees across five countries, 80+ financial partners, and platforms serving 1M+ users.


We're builders: outcome-driven, fast-moving, and close to the business. You'll have real autonomy to explore new technologies and shape how we work.


We offer competitive compensation, wellness benefits, and the possibility to work in a hybrid setup.  

This is a full-time position. The current tech team for Power & Protect is based in Jönköping & Helsingborg. We are now looking to hire in Helsingborg.  

  

We are located at Hetch in the Prisma building right next to Helsingborg central station. The workspace have all the bells and whistles you may need - Modern office, terrace overlooking Öresund, restaurant with excellent food and an outdoor gym at the rooftop.


Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis - **we look forward to hearing from you!**

title: UX Designer/User Clinic Research
description:
**ABOUT ENGAGE STUDIOS**


We design, innovate and build digital / physical products for the world's largest - and Sweden's smallest company. Our guiding principle is to make a difference - for the better. Ideas are good and execution is the success factor. We are people and teams who are not afraid to question, roll up our sleeves and laugh all the way to the finish line.


Since the start in 2009, we have worked with basically all manufacturing / developing companies in Sweden. The Volvo companies, IKEA, H&M, Electrolux, Lynk&Co, Zeekr, Houdini to name a few. Our customers are in the traditional Swedish industry all the way to innovative startups in Silicon Valley. They are not found in oil drilling or the military industry. We are determined to make our presence and work known with passionate and dedicated employees who love design, tech and people.

 **WE OFFER**


At Engage Studios, you will work in many different industries for different customers. You will work with incredibly smart and dedicated colleagues. Together we develop products and services that you can brag about to your friends. We at Engage Studios are always at the forefront of technology development. We develop and share knowledge with each other to get better tomorrow. With us, you get the opportunity to realize your dreams, big and small.

 **JOB DESCRIPTION**

* Support the creation of UX concepts, wireframes and interface design for infotainment and digital vehicle functions.
* Assist in feature development and design improvements based on user needs, customer feedback and market requirements.
* Participate in user research, user clinics and usability evaluations to identify insights and improvement opportunities.
* Collaborate with developers, product owners and validation teams to support implementation and ensure UX quality.
* Contribute to benchmarking and competitor analysis of automotive UX/UI systems and experiences.
* Support design reviews, workshops and stakeholder presentations to communicate UX concepts and findings.

 **REQUIREMENTS**

* Some experience (e.g. from internships, student projects or 0–2 years in industry) as a UX Designer,


Interaction Designer or similar role in digital product development.

* Basic understanding of user-centered design methods, wireframing and prototyping.
* Good basic understanding of UX principles, usability and interaction design, and a strong willingness to learn.
* Interest in user research, usability testing and/or customer feedback analysis (hands-on experience is a plus).
* Good communication and collaboration skills in cross-functional teams.
* Experience working with Figma or similar UX/UI design tools.
* Experience in the automotive industry is considered an advantage.

**YOUR PROFILE**

* We are looking for an individual in the early stage of their UX career who is curious, proactive and eager to learn.
* You can manage your tasks with guidance, drive issues with energy, be transparent when reporting status, and collaborate effectively across functions.


 If you are passionate about development, we want to hear from you. Please apply with your resume, portfolio, and a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and qualifications.

**MERITS**

* Visually communicative
* Tons of Passion

**PLACE OF EMPLOYMENT**


Gothenburg

 **OTHER**


For questions regarding this position please contact hiring manager Susanne Lidén on susanne@engagestudios.com. Applications must be submitted through the official Career site.

title: Senior Product Designer
description:
MyCamper is the largest sharing platform for camping vehicles in Switzerland, Sweden, Norway, Finland and Denmark, and growing in Germany and Austria. Over 5,000 vehicles, profitable since 2024, around 20 people across Zurich, Uppsala and Mostar. We're on a mission to make camping accessible for everyone by making better use of existing resources.


The product team is around nine people, roughly half the company: designers, PMs, engineers, and the Head of Product, working closely together with no handoff layer. Decisions are collaborative; the designer leads the call on design. We're data-driven by default and judge work by outcomes rather than artifacts.


As Senior Product Designer you own the user experience of our marketplace end to end, on both sides. On the renter side, that means making search, booking, listings, communication and handover feel as easy as booking an Airbnb. On the owner side, it means giving owners clarity on pricing, availability, who's renting, and what happens when things go wrong: confidence drives supply, and supply drives the business. UI craft is the baseline; UX thinking across the whole journey is the job.

### **Tasks**

* Own design end-to-end across the rental and renting-out experiences: search, booking, listings, communication, handover, post-trip.
* Lead design reviews; make the call on direction, in collaboration with the rest of the product team.
* Own and evolve the MyCamper design system so Figma and production speak the same language.
* Watch how users behave in production after release, and iterate based on what you see.

### **Requirements**

* **Proven impact at a previous company.** This is a senior role and we'll judge it that way. You can point to specific outcomes you drove ("I moved that"), not just artifacts in a portfolio.
* **A strong UX brain, evidence-based by default.** You design from the user's problem, not from a screen. You run interviews, query data, validate assumptions with experiments. When data is missing, you propose a hypothesis and the cheapest test, not a research project.
* **Strong UI craft, as a baseline.** The interfaces you ship look and feel right: consistent, intentional, appropriate to context. We wouldn't hire someone who isn't at least good at UI; we just don't think it's the differentiator.
* **Agency.** You see a problem, you bring a path forward. You don't wait to be told. You raise blockers immediately, drive the agenda in syncs, and call out hacks vs. real solutions.
* **Sharp design judgment, grounded in reference.** Before generating solutions you look at how others in the P2P-sharing or travel-tech space have solved a similar problem. You frame in context before designing in a vacuum.
* **Design system ownership.** You've built or meaningfully maintained one. A design system isn't only for designers; it's for developers, so they can build consistently at speed.
* **AI-curious.** You don't need to be fluent on day one, but designing with AI as a thinking partner should excite you, not intimidate you.

### **Nice to have**

* Marketplace background, or experience at another product company.
* Hands-on with BigQuery, PostHog, or similar product analytics tools yourself. Genuinely valued, not taken for granted.
* Already fluent in AI-assisted prototyping (Claude, FigmaMake, v0, similar).
* P2P-sharing or travel-tech experience specifically.

### **How we work**

**Data-driven by default.** Everyone in the company has access to production and marketing data through BigQuery and Claude. We judge work by outcomes (metric movement, user behavior), not by output. When data doesn't exist, we run experiments to generate it.

**Real artifacts beat polished slides.** Designers and PMs increasingly vibe-code interactive prototypes with Claude and FigmaMake rather than building static Figma frames. Clickable beats polished; thinking together with something on the screen is faster than thinking alone and presenting later.

**Design decisions are collaborative; the designer leads.** The team reviews designs together. On design calls, the designer takes the lead and makes the call, always in collab with the team, but the call is yours.

**The design system is the substrate.** It's what makes speed and quality compatible. New features get built from it, not around it. Drift gets flagged before it becomes a precedent.

### **Who you'd work with**


You'd report to **Nils** (Head of Product). Your peers are **Tine** (Designer) and **Rickard** (Product Manager). Engineers, designers and PMs run as one product team: joint standups and retros, no handoff layer.

### **Benefits**

* Chance to shape a fast growing international tech startup and become part of MyCamper's success story.
* Flat hierarchies in a motivated, dynamic, and ambitious team across 3 locations (Uppsala Sweden, Zürich Switzerland, Mostar Bosnia-Herzegovina).
* Regular international team events in one of our locations.
* 4 weeks working from anywhere and 40% home office.
* 50% discount on camper rentals through MyCamper.
* 1000 CHF perks budget per year that can be used for education or personal health.

### **Logistics**

* **Full-time, permanent.**
* **Uppsala-based.** We prefer co-location with the product team. This is not a remote role.
* **Salary.** 40-50k SEK/month, plus pension.

### **How to apply**


Send your CV, a short cover letter in English, and 2-3 pieces of work where you drove real impact (with the outcome, not just the screens). We'll get back to you within seven days.

**A note on freelancers and agencies**


We're hiring a permanent in-house designer, not contracting work out. We believe in building a strong, consistent team that grows and learns together over time, and that's the bet we're making with this role. If you're a freelancer or agency, thanks for thinking of us; this one won't be a fit.


Our 20 people live in Uppsala, Zurich, and Mostar, with roots across Northern, Central, and Southeastern Europe. We don't optimize for sameness; we hire for people who care about the craft and own the outcome.

title: UI Programmer, Crusader Kings 3
description:
**About the role**
------------------


We are looking for a UI Programmer to join the UX team on Crusader Kings III. In this role you will be the bridge between design and game, turning UX concepts and UI art into interfaces that make CK3's deep systems feel inviting and clear to players. You will work across both the C++ game layer and our in-house UI scripting language, and collaborate closely with designers, artists, and programmers throughout every feature cycle.

**What you will be doing**
--------------------------

* Design and implement user interfaces - text displays, HUDs, menus, and in-game elements - from concept to shipped feature
* Use our in-house UI scripting language to build interfaces that reflect design intent and connect to the game API
* Implement game-side data models that feed the UI layer
* Debug and maintain the UI to ensure a stable, performant product across supported platforms
* Collaborate closely with UX designers, UI artists, gameplay programmers, and audio throughout the full feature cycle
* Participate in estimations, task breakdowns, code reviews, and retrospectives
* Contribute to the project's UI style guide, design patterns, and shared components, and help teammates grow in UI scripting
* Take on non-UI tasks when the project needs it

**What we are looking for**
---------------------------

**Required**

* Professional experience as a UI programmer, gameplay programmer with UI focus, or similar role in game development
* Ability to learn and become productive in new scripting languages, including domain-specific UI scripting languages
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
* A passion for making interfaces accessible to a broad player base
* Genuine interest in UI and game design, and enjoyment of close collaboration with designers and artists

 **Nice to have**

* Experience with C++ in a game development context
* Strong knowledge of UI design patterns
* Experience with UI performance profiling and optimization
* Familiarity with Paradox Development Studio games, especially Crusader Kings III, as a player or modder
* Interest in history, particularly the medieval period

**Who you are**
---------------


You care about the player experience and are driven to iterate until something feels right. You are positive, responsible, and enjoy the challenge of translating complex systems into clear, satisfying interfaces. You thrive in a collaborative environment where designers, artists, and engineers work closely together.

**The team**
------------


You will be part of the UX Design discipline on Crusader Kings III, reporting to the Managing Design Lead. Your closest day-to-day collaborators will be UX designers and UI artists, with frequent work alongside gameplay programmers, game designers, audio, QA, and production.

**Type of Employment:** Full time, permanent (6 months probation)

**Location:** Stockholm (fully onsite with flexibility)

title: UX Designer
description:
As a UX Designer you will be working on one of our Game Projects in a cross-disciplinary team, taking ownership for UX design of features to improve the end-user experience. You will be part of shipping new features, and balancing maintenance of existing features for live service games.
   

  

 To thrive in this role we think you have experience working with complex products and designing for different platforms and input methods such as PC, console or mobile. Previous experience in working with games is a plus.
   

  

**Example Of Responsibilities**
* Collaborate across disciplines and align stakeholders to define clear problem statements, user needs and design solutions to solve those.
* Own the user experience of various features end-to-end, from defining problems and needs iterating all the way until they are in the players’ hands.
* Design using user flows, wireframes and prototypes, that adhere to the game’s design system and style guides.
* Work on a range of experiences, from crafting systems to character customization, based on where we can have the biggest impact.
* Work proactively, identifying gaps in the user experience to inform design decisions
* Consider systems before individual screens. Consider edge cases early, flexible design solutions, and aim for coherency in design choices.
* Deliver and explore many possible solutions, while able to narrow down and pivot with relevant feedback.
* Contribute and foster a culture of sharing and feedback on design.
* Leverage the team’s combined expertise in finding the best solutions and increase the knowledge across the studio.


**We would love if you have**
* A creative and curious mind motivated by understanding user needs and how to fill those to improve the experience.
* Strong communication skills to clearly convey design decisions, and doing so early and frequently under development.
* Experience in using both qualitative and quantitative data to understand user needs and finding possible design solutions.
* Experience with different platforms and input methods, such as focus-based input.
* Ability to work in a fast-paced environment with shifting priorities for the biggest impact.
* An interest in games and player experiences.
* 4+ years of experience in UX/UI or product design.
* A portfolio with relevant projects showcasing your design process and impact.
* Fluency in English.


 At Embark we offer competitive salaries, passionate colleagues to share knowledge with and much more, but most of all we invite you to take part of a journey into the unknown, to build creative, surprising and beautiful experiences together.
   

  

 Be yourself at Embark and make games while doing so. Please apply with confidence. We can’t wait to hear from you (in English)!

title: Digital Creative Strategist
description:
Commercial Development & Growth is a global function within the H&M Brand. Our mission is to define and deliver the global customer experience strategy - strengthening our brand while enabling profitable growth. Together, we develop, expand and optimise all sales channels, creating the conditions for efficient, tech-enabled operations through strong cross-functional collaboration.
   

  





 We play a key role in bringing the brand plan to life by shaping an elevated, inspirational and relevant shopping experience. By putting our product at the heart of the journey, we ensure a seamless and competitive experience for our customers.
   

  





 Within this, the Creative Development department delivers a cohesive brand experience across all channels - anchored in our identity and guided by a clear, consistent and creative vision.
   

  

  

  




**Job Description**

**WHAT YOU’LL DO**




 We’re now looking for a Digital Creative Strategist to join the Creative Development team and shape the direction of our digital store experience. In this role, you’ll connect strategy with creativity - turning ambition into clear, compelling creative concepts and ideas that drive brand impact, user engagement and commercial success.
   

  





*You will:*



* Frame ambition into clear creative direction
	+ Develop clear creative briefs that clarify scope, ambition, and priorities
	+ Translate strategic input into actionable creative direction that guides digital initiative
	+ Define experience principles and creative guardrails with the overall brand direction


* Shape the creative ambition and expression
	+ Develop core creative concepts, narrative direction, and emotional ambition for key initiatives
	+ Translate creative intent into clear design principles guiding visual identity and storytelling
	+ Make informed creative recommendations balancing brand ambition, user experience, and commercial impact
* Secure quality and coherence in execution
	+ Apply and uphold creative principles to ensure consistency and distinctiveness across products and platforms
	+ Provide creative leadership to internal teams and external partners during concept and design development
	+ Ensure execution aligns with the defined creative direction


**Qualifications**

 WHO YOU ARE
   

  





*We are looking for people with…*



* 8+ years experience in digital creative or design roles
* A degree in Design, Communication, Strategy, or a related field
* High design judgement across digital UI/UX and visual systems
* The ability to balance brand ambition, usability, and commercial impact
* Clear communication skills across Creative, Product, and Commercial teams



*And people who are…*



* Confident in shaping both strategic framing and creative direction within product-led environments
* Collaborative, with a proactive and solution-focused mindset
* Comfortable leading creative conversations and influencing stakeholders
* Strategic thinkers and conceptually strong


**Additional Information**

 This is a
 **full time permanent position** 
 based in Stockholm at our offices. Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through our career page.
   

  





**WHO WE ARE** 
**​** 





 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​
   

  





 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​
   

  





 ​
 **WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE** 
**​** 
  

  

 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​
   

  




* 25% Staff discount on all our H&M Group brands, both in stores and online (H&M, COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories and ARKET).
* H&M Incentive Program – HIP. Learn more about the program here.
* In addition to our global benefits, our local markets offer different competitive perks and benefits. (Example: Competitive compensation for your dedication and long-term commitment. A day off on your birthday to relax and celebrate your special day. Special gift to celebrate the birth of your child, etc.)



**JOIN US** 
**​** 





 Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.​
   

  





 Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.​
   

  





 ​​We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.​

title: UX designer, Ad Solutions
description:
**The Company**
 Schibsted is a Nordic digital company with leading positions in news media and digital services. Every day, millions of people use our products to stay informed, make decisions, and connect with the world around them.
   

  

**Strategic purpose of the role**
 As UX Designer, Ad Solutions, you support the development and evolution of Admarket — Schibsted’s advertising platform — by ensuring that we deliver a clear, intuitive, and effective user experience.
   

  

 Working closely with product managers and engineers, you translate product goals and requirements into user-centred design solutions. Through research, ideation, and design, you help ensure that solutions address real user needs while aligning with business objectives and technical constraints.
   

  

 You contribute throughout the product development process — from discovery and concept exploration to detailed design and iterative improvements — helping the team deliver high-quality experiences that are scalable, consistent, and easy to use.
   

  

**Role Tasks And Responsibilities**
* Support product and platform development through research, ideation, and design
* Collaborate with product managers and engineers to identify problems and shape solutions
* Explore and validate design solutions through sketches, prototypes, or interactive concepts using the most effective tools available
* Deliver clear specifications and documentation to support engineering implementation
* Contribute to maintaining and evolving the design system
* Evaluate and improve existing user experiences through research, analytics, and feedback
* Ensure that designs meet usability, accessibility, and quality standards
* Experiment with new tools and workflows — including AI-assisted prototyping or rapid concept builds — to explore and validate solutions faster


**Challenges in the role / special complexity**
* Designing solutions for a platform used by multiple user groups with different needs and workflows
* Balancing usability, business goals, and technical constraints when shaping design solutions
* Working closely with product managers and engineers in a fast-moving, iterative product environment
* Ensuring consistency across the platform through shared patterns and design system practices while supporting ongoing product evolution
* Adapting design workflows and methods as new tools and technologies enable faster exploration and validation of solutions
* Developing domain understanding of the advertising ecosystem, including how digital ads are bought, managed, and served across the platform


**Hard Skills**
* Professional proficiency in English and either Swedish or Norwegian is required (written and spoken)
* Experience designing digital products, ideally for web-based platforms or complex systems
* Strong skills in user research using both qualitative and quantitative methods (e.g. interviews, usability testing, surveys, and analytics tools such as Amplitude)
* Solid understanding of interaction design, user flows, and visual design principles
* Ability to translate product requirements and user insights into clear, usable design solutions
* Experience working in cross-functional product teams together with product managers and engineers
* Proficiency with modern design and collaboration tools (e.g. Figma and related tools)
* Knowledge of accessibility principles and how to apply them in product design


**Soft Skills**
* User-centred – Focuses on understanding user needs and improving the overall experience
* Collaborative – Works effectively with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders across the organisation
* Structured – Organises design work clearly and communicates solutions in a structured way
* Curious – Interested in learning from users, data, and new technologies
* Communicative – Able to clearly explain design decisions and gather input from


 others
   

  

**We value curiosity, drive, and a willingness to learn. Although you may not meet every requirement, this role is best suited to designers with experience working on digital products in cross-functional product teams. If that sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you.**

title: Junior Web Designer
description:
**About Eklips** 
  
 



  
 



 Eklips Digital is the Nordic leader in subscription-based investor and corporate websites for publicly listed companies. We've delivered 300+ sites since 2011 and serve around 10% of all companies on Nasdaq Stockholm's Main Market. Our team of 14 consists of designers, developers, and specialists with deep experience in investor and corporate communication. We're part of Modular Finance Group, alongside Modular Finance and Börsdata. 
 



  
 



 We are now entering an ambitious growth phase. At the same time, the investor and corporate websites have never been more important. It is where investors, media and the market form their view of a company, and AI-driven search raises the bar further, with websites increasingly the source that AI tools read and summarise. Design plays a big role in making this work, and you'll help shape our vision for it. 
 



  
 



**The role** 
  
 



  
 



 You'll join our small design team and work side by side with our Senior Designer, with the wider team of developers and Customer Success team members. We value clear thinking over loud voices, and you'll always have sounding boards close by. 
 



  
 



 We deliver investor and corporate websites as a subscription service: onboarding new customers, getting their sites live, and supporting them as they grow. Your day is hands-on design work, turning our templates and design system into great websites for the listed companies we serve. On the customer side, you'll work with the corporate communications and investor relations teams and/or sometimes the communications advisory firms that support them. 
 



  
 



 This is a role to grow in. You'll start by supporting more experienced colleagues and taking ownership of defined tasks, and step by step you'll take on more of your own. 
 



  
 



**What you'll do** 
  
 



  
 


* Support our senior designer in design deliveries from concept to final delivery
* Work hands-on with our templating and design system, the components and layouts behind the sites we build, and help develop and refine them over time
* Take part in planning sessions, internally and with customers, to shape better outcomes
* Help shape how we deliver design, with new tools, AI-assisted workflows, and approaches that scale across our customer base



  
 



**You’ll thrive here if** 
  
 



  
 


* You're early in your career as a web, UX or UI designer, perhaps through education, internships, or your own projects. More importantly, you're motivated to keep growing your craft.
* You're organised, you communicate clearly and enjoy talking through the thinking behind your design choices and you're open to sharing your own and hearing other perspectives.
* You have a portfolio that shows strong visual sensibility, attention to detail, and how you think through a design problem.
* You're comfortable having parallel projects on the go at the same time, and can ask for help when priorities need sorting out.
* You're curious about technology. You like trying new tools, experimenting with AI in your workflow, and you see new technology as an opportunity rather than a threat
* Basic HTML and CSS, awareness of WCAG and accessibility, and an interest in the stock market and how listed companies communicate are all pluses, not requirements.



  
 



 If you recognise yourself in most of this, we'd love to hear from you.  
 



  
 



**What we offer** 
  
 



  
 


* A close-knit team and experienced colleagues who genuinely want you to grow. Questions are welcome, and no one's expected to know everything from day one.
* A workplace that fosters learning and knowledge sharing within our field.
* A fast-growing company where you'll find real opportunities to develop.
* The stability of being part of Modular Finance Group.
* A fixed salary, occupational pension, and wellness allowance.
* A flat culture where we make decisions based on reasoning, not titles.



  
 



**How we hire** 
  
 



  
 



 We aim to hire based on how you think, not how you perform under interview pressure. That's why two things matter most to us: your portfolio, and a short design test assignment that final candidates complete as part of the process. Both give you a real chance to show how you approach a design problem, and give us a genuine feel for how you work. 
 



  
 



**Application** 
  
 



  
 



 Send your CV and a link to your portfolio to jobs@eklips.se. If your portfolio is password-protected, remember to include the login details.  
 



  
 



 Don't wait too long — we review applications on a rolling basis.

title: KICKS söker vikarierande Motion & Digital Designer
description:
Är du en kreativ Motion & Digital Designer med stark känsla för rörligt innehåll, digital design och dagens medielandskap? Nu söker vi dig som vill vara med och stärka KICKS under ett vikariat från mitten av augusti 2026 till juni 2027.
   

  

 I rollen ansvarar du för att utveckla och producera rörligt och digitalt material för KICKS kampanjer och kommunikation i våra olika kanaler. Du arbetar nära Art Directors, Copywriters, projektledare och kanalägare för att skapa innehåll som är visuellt starkt, kanaloptimerat och affärsdrivande.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har ett starkt intresse för rörliga format och digital kommunikation och som förstår hur innehåll behöver anpassas för olika plattformar, målgrupper och medieköp. Du har gärna erfarenhet från reklambyrå eller inhousebyrå och drivs av att skapa material som bryter igenom bruset.
   

  

 Vi befinner oss i en spännande förändringsresa där AI är en viktig del av vårt arbetssätt, och vi ser gärna att du har både vana och intresse av att använda AI som stöd i produktion och kreativa processer.
   

  

**Exempel på arbetsuppgifter:**
* Producera och utveckla rörligt material för digitala kanaler såsom paid social, DOOH, digitala butiksskärmar och sociala medier.
* Skapa, anpassa och vidareutveckla material genom animation, klippning och formatanpassning utifrån befintligt ID och guidelines.
* Säkerställa att material är optimerat för respektive kanal, plattform och målgrupp.
* Samarbeta nära kreativa team och kanalfunktioner för att skapa relevant och effektiv kommunikation.
* Bidra med kunskap kring rörligt innehåll, digitala format och utvecklingen inom dagens medialandskap.
* Stötta i hantering och uppladdning av material till digitala skärmar och andra digitala plattformar.


**Vem vi söker:**
* En tekniskt skicklig och kreativ Motion & Digital Designer med stark känsla för rörligt innehåll.
* Har minst 3 års erfarenhet från reklambyrå, inhousebyrå eller liknande kreativ miljö.
* Har mycket god förståelse för digitala kanaler, sociala medier och hur innehåll anpassas för olika medieformat.
* Van att arbeta i högt tempo med flera parallella projekt.
* Prestigelös, lösningsorienterad och en stark lagspelare.
* Har mycket goda kunskaper inom Adobe Creative Cloud, särskilt After Effects, Premiere Pro, Photoshop och Illustrator samt Figma.
* Erfarenhet av AI-verktyg inom produktion och design är meriterande.


**Om tjänsten**
 Detta är ett vikariat från mitten av augusti 2026 till juni 2027. Du blir en del av vår inhousebyrå i moderna lokaler vid Klarabergsviadukten i Stockholm.
   

  

 Låter det här spännande? Intervjuer sker löpande och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas snabbt – skicka in din ansökan redan idag! För utvalda kandidater kan processen följas av arbetsprov.

title: Motion Designer
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is redefining how legal work gets done. Not built for lawyers, built with them. We work alongside the world’s best legal teams, who expect excellence, precision, and speed, and we hold ourselves to the same bar.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace lets legal professionals move faster, think more clearly, and operate with sharper precision. By analysing thousands of documents in minutes and powering end-to-end workflows, we cut through complexity, teams can focus on what matters: judgment, strategy, and outcomes.
   

  

 1,000+ customers across 50+ countries trust us, including Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Linklaters, White & Case, Dentons, and Barclays. We’ve scaled to
 **$100M+ in ARR** 
 , with teams across Europe, North America and APAC, and continue to expand through acquisitions including Qura, Walter AI and Graceview.
   

  

 We partner with world-class performers: including Aaron Judge and the New York Yankees, Ludvig Åberg (and his caddie), and campaigns featuring Jude Law.
   

  

 Joining Legora means three things.
   

  

* We lean in: ownership over titles, outcomes over intentions.
* We fight for excellence: high standards, direct, ego-free feedback.
* We grow together: as a team and with our customers.


 Mission before ego. Everyone contributes. No one coasts.
   

  

 If you’re driven by impact, pace, and raising the bar. This is the place.
   

  

 As a Motion Designer on the Design team, you will bring the Legora brand to life through motion, animation, and storytelling. You’ll craft motion systems that scale across channels, product marketing, social, campaigns, ads, and events, and help define how we use movement to communicate clearly and feel alive. You’ll work closely with Marketing to concept, build, and ship compelling creative work that captures attention and elevates our brand in a B2B context.
   

  

 We’re looking for a motion designer with strong execution skills and a sharp design eye, someone who sees motion as a tool for clarity, emotion, and momentum.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Design and animate motion assets for product launches, campaigns, social, explainers, and ads
* Build a scalable motion system and toolkit to ensure quality and consistency at speed
* Support major product launches with high-quality motion
* Collaborate with Marketing and other designers to explore and ship motion-driven work


**Who You Are**
* A motion-first designer with a strong foundation in visual design and storytelling
* Portfolio that showcases high-quality motion work across brand, product, and marketing
* Expert in tools like After Effects, Premiere, Illustrator, Jitter, and Figma
* Comfortable working fast and iterating based on feedback
* Experience designing for B2B or tech companies
* Early adopter of AI tools to accelerate creative workflows


**What’s In It For You**
* Career development opportunities in a rapidly evolving entrepreneurial environment
* A collaborative, high-energy team environment where your ideas and contributions are valued and implemented
* Competitive salary complemented with a transparent and highly competitive options program
* Centrally located offices in Stockholm, designed as a space for you to do your life’s work


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Brand Design Director
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is redefining how legal work gets done. Not built for lawyers, built with them. We work alongside the world’s best legal teams, who expect excellence, precision, and speed, and we hold ourselves to the same bar.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace lets legal professionals move faster, think more clearly, and operate with sharper precision. By analysing thousands of documents in minutes and powering end-to-end workflows, we cut through complexity, teams can focus on what matters: judgment, strategy, and outcomes.
   

  

 1,000+ customers across 50+ countries trust us, including Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Linklaters, White & Case, Dentons, and Barclays. We’ve scaled to
 **$100M+ in ARR** 
 , with teams across Europe, North America and APAC, and continue to expand through acquisitions including Qura, Walter AI and Graceview.
   

  

 We partner with world-class performers: including Aaron Judge and the New York Yankees, Ludvig Åberg (and his caddie), and campaigns featuring Jude Law.
   

  

 Joining Legora means three things.
   

  

* We lean in: ownership over titles, outcomes over intentions.
* We fight for excellence: high standards, direct, ego-free feedback.
* We grow together: as a team and with our customers.


 Mission before ego. Everyone contributes. No one coasts.
   

  

 If you’re driven by impact, pace, and raising the bar. This is the place.
   

  

 The Role
   

  

 We’re hiring a Brand Design Director to lead our brand designers and shape everything visual and experiential about Legora. This is a senior creative leadership role reporting to the Brand Lead, based in-person at our Stockholm HQ.
   

  

 You’ll set the design direction across brand, product marketing, campaigns, and content, building a creative identity that matches the ambition of the product. Working closely with the Brand Lead, you’ll help evolve the overall brand vision while collaborating with marketing, product, design and leadership to shape how Legora shows up in the world.
   

  

 This is an end-to-end role where you’ll lead our brand designers, oversee day-to-day design execution in close collaboration with the Production Manager, and ensure a consistently high creative bar across everything we produce. You’ll also support the sourcing and management of external partners, including agencies, freelancers, and studios when needed.
   

  

 What You'll Own
   

  

**The brand design team.** 
 Hire, mentor, and develop a world-class brand-design function. Create the conditions for people to do the best work of their careers.
   

  

**Creative direction.** 
 Define and evolve Legora's visual identity, brand system, and design language. Set the standard for everything we produce, from product pages to pitch decks to events. Make it unmistakably Legora.
   

  

**External creative partnerships.** 
 Source and lead agencies, freelancers, and production partners. Set the brief, steer the work, and ensure exceptional output.
   

  

**Campaigns and content.** 
 Work closely with the Brand team to define campaign and content concepts, and bring these ideas to life together with the broader Marketing team across digital, social, events, and other marketing channels.
   

  

**Brand coherence.** 
 Work closely with Design leadership in Product as part of an internal Design Council to ensure Legora shows up consistently, from the first touchpoint through the in-product experience.
   

  

**AI-enabled creativity.** 
 Stay at the frontier of AI and emerging creative tools. Apply them in ways that unlock new creative capabilities across the company.
   

  

**Craft.** 
 Set a high bar for quality. Know when to push further and when to ship.
   

  

 What We're Looking For
   

  

* 10+ years in creative roles, with at least 3 years leading a team. You started as a hands-on designer and still know your way around the tools.
* A portfolio that shows range: brand systems, digital campaigns, product marketing, content, experiential.
* Proven experience sourcing, briefing, and managing external creative partners (agencies, studios, freelancers).
* Strong point of view on design and brand, with the skill to articulate it clearly and bring others along.
* Proven track record of building and leading creative teams, from hiring and mentoring talent to creating the structures that enable teams to do their best work.
* Comfort operating in a fast-moving, high-growth environment. You know how to prioritize ruthlessly and ship work that matters.
* Proven experience working within B2B, SaaS, or technology environments.


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Lead Product Manager: Ecosystem Social Mini Games
description:
**Description & Requirements**
 Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.
   

  

 This role will be on site/hybrid, and can be out of either location listed.
   

  

 Join us at Electronic Arts, a global leader inspiring the world to play through innovative and inclusive entertainment. As Lead Product Manager for our In-Game Experiences team, you will guide an early-stage program focused on daily puzzle-based games that deepen player connection and expand engagement across mobile, web, and EA platforms.
   

  

 Reporting to the Director of Product, you will shape the vision, strategy, and roadmap for a new experience at the intersection of games, social engagement, and ecosystem growth, collaborating with us to deliver meaningful impact for millions of players.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* You will translate strategic goals into a clear product vision, roadmap, and measurable KPIs, aligning partners across EA and representing key updates to our organization.
* You will lead product discovery by validating player needs, conducting competitive analysis, tracking market trends, and developing business cases to inform investment decisions.
* You will define the end-to-end player experience, including daily game loops, meta progression, and social systems such as leaderboards, streaks, achievements, and sharing across multiple surfaces.
* You will establish a test-iterate-learn process with Analytics and Research, using experimentation, telemetry, and player feedback to improve engagement, retention, and product-market fit.


**Required Qualifications**
* 5+ years of product management or game producer experience leading consumer-facing digital products in gaming, social platforms, mobile, web, or live services.
* Experience guiding 0 to 1 product discovery, definition, and delivery, including concept validation, prototyping, and launch for high-impact projects.
* Experience setting success metrics, defining KPIs, and using data-driven experimentation to improve player engagement and business outcomes.
* Experience collaborating with Design, Engineering, Analytics, and Research partners to deliver technically complex initiatives that connect ecosystem value and deepen player relationships.


 We want to connect you with job opportunities that align with your interests, skills, and expertise. When you create an EA Careers Account and are logged into the portal, you can click “Get Recommendations” to view a curated list of job openings. These recommendations are enhanced by automated processing, including artificial intelligence, and take into account your skills and experience. However, all employment decisions are made by our hiring teams, not by automated systems.
   

  

**Pay Transparency - North America**
**Compensation And Benefits**
 The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).
   

  

**PAY RANGES**
* Ontario \*$141,400 - $192,100 CAD


 Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.
   

  

 For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.
   

  

 This role is for a newly-created position.
   

  

**About Electronic Arts**
 We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.
   

  

 We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life.
